title: Unable to run `podman pod create` on systems without systemd installed.
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When attempting to run `podman pod create` on systems without systemd installed, such as on a system with OpenRC as the init system, podman errors with `unable to create pod cgroup for pod`. Setting cgroups-manager to cgroupfs and systemd to false in containers.conf does not fix the issue.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run `podman pod create` as root on OpenRC system

**Describe the results you received:**

Error `unable to create pod cgroup for pod ... The name org.freedesktop.systemd1 was not provided by any .service files`

**Describe the results you expected:**

cgroup creation using cgroupfs/related tools rather than systemd 

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.6
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.15
Git Commit:   27362ba1ad8879ea71610fa68a651a1651e0180f
Built:        Tue Sep  1 15:43:39 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**
Yes this is latest version, and I have checked the guide
Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Can you try using `--cgroup-manager=cgroupfs` and see if it works?
Ah, sorry, I see you already mentioned this doesn't work. Interesting...

It seems that `cgroup-manager=cgroupfs` was not being applied in `/etc/containers/containers.conf`, but is applied properly when put in `/etc/containers/libpod.conf`

I'm sure I just got mixed up somewhere in the docs on what to put where, or didn't fully understand how to utilize containers.conf - sorry for the hassle!

Hm, now when `podman pod create` is run as it's own command, it works fine, but when podman-compose runs it, it errors.

Not sure what's happening here.

EDIT:

According to the `containers.conf` manpage, my big error was forgetting the `[engine]` section header

And according to [this issue](https://github.com/containers/podman-compose/issues/209) it seems the current stable release of podman-compose was not reading the containers.conf

Okay, I think I've isolated the issue - rootless runs the `pod create` with no issue, but root does not.

---

--- 

Only configuration file named libpod.conf or container.conf on my system is the `/etc/containers/containers.conf` file that has the text

```
[engine]
cgroup-manager = "cgroupfs"
systemd = "false"
```
Not yet, I will try and take a look this week.

Initial impression: root invocation is definitely not using `cgroupfs` but rather systemd cgroups. Can you confirm that you are definitely passing `--cgroup-manager=cgroupfs` to the root `podman pod create` invocation?

Manual invocation of `podman pod create --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs` as root works as expected.

The issue arises when other programs run podman create with commands I can't manually control, I'm forced to use configuration files - files which root invocation of `podman pod create` seems to be ignoring when it comes to cgroup-manager

It's ignoring `containers.conf`?

Can you provide the contents of the configuration file(s) you're using? Can you try making other changes and seeing if they work?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we got no info back from reporter, closing.  Reopen if there is new info.
Excellent point. I imagine this will work if the correct key is used.

A small fix: @xals is right, but he meant `cgroup_manager` not `cgroup_engine`, I think.

ie, you want:

```
[engine]
cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs"
```

I'm having a similar issue on a Devuan GNU/Linux 4.

I installed podman by simply using the apt package manager:

```
apt install podman
```

I tried to setup the `/etc/containers/containers.conf` as mentioned above (it didn't exist, I created it), without much success:

```
 # root@latitude: ~ < 2023_06_18__12:00:36 > [bashpid_5652 25]
podman run -dt -p 8080:80/tcp docker.io/library/httpd
ERRO[0000] unable to write pod event: "write unixgram @00055->/run/systemd/journal/socket: sendmsg: no such file or directory" 
ERRO[0000] unable to write pod event: "write unixgram @00055->/run/systemd/journal/socket: sendmsg: no such file or directory" 
ERRO[0000] unable to write pod event: "write unixgram @00055->/run/systemd/journal/socket: sendmsg: no such file or directory" 
5ca14a75edf14e1d2ded04631a81b95497572320cb71cffacbfedd81bb84d279
```

Maybe I'm missing something?
